brief_justification: Arrives at the exact correct answer (B) with valid chronological enumeration and correct cutoff application; goes one level beyond by noting T2 cannot safely read T1 since majority isn't reached until after T2 starts, but doesn't explore further second-order effects; explicitly names the suppressed failure mode inline ('T2's start time ≠ T1's commit time'), demonstrating active self-monitoring; applies ✓/✗ checks per node against the cutoff timestamp but does not recheck from a different angle or test boundary conditions; correctly distinguishes committed vs. in-progress state but doesn't quantify confidence or state what assumptions could change the answer; implicitly rejects (C) by noting majority isn't reached until .200, but doesn't systematically address all four options; full sorted table plus per-node cutoff verification gives a reproducible derivation chain; explicitly avoids the anti-pattern of trusting presentation order by re-sorting and extracting each timestamp independently; avoids assumed ordering acceptance and clock trust heuristic, explicitly flagging the commit-vs-start confusion; reasoning follows the extract → sort → gate(timestamp ≤ query?) → validated-count path of the topology but does not name branches or gates explicitly.
